L1.area: Data persistence and file loading infrastructure

L2.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where the default configuration allows deserialization of arbitrary Python objects from crafted serialized data files. When a user loads data from an untrusted source, attacker-controlled serialized objects can trigger arbitrary code execution without any explicit opt-in from the caller.

L3.area: NumPy's binary array file format loading routines for .npy and .npz files
L3.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where loading NumPy's binary array file formats from untrusted sources permits arbitrary code execution. The default configuration invokes Python pickle deserialization without requiring explicit opt-in from the caller, allowing crafted serialized object arrays embedded in these file formats to execute arbitrary code upon loading.

ground_truth.reason: NumPy's data loading functions default to allow_pickle=True, enabling deserialization of arbitrary Python objects via pickle. An attacker can craft a malicious .npy or .npz file that executes arbitrary code when loaded.
